description: a4/ workspace hooks. Most logic lives in the Python dispatcher `scripts/a4_hook.py` (subcommands: post-edit, stop, session-start, session-end). Two small bash hooks (`cleanup-edited-a4.sh`, `sweep-old-edited-a4.sh`) handle trivial edited-record tmp-file lifecycle; the parallel workflow-mode lifecycle (`scripts/workflow_mode.py` init/sweep at SessionStart, cleanup at SessionEnd) runs in-process from the dispatcher. Session-scoped edit tracking at `.claude/tmp/a4-edited/a4-edited-<session_id>.txt` lets Stop validate only this session's edits. SessionStart initializes workflow-mode state (`conversational` default), sweeps stale workflow-mode files, refreshes UC `implemented_by:` reverse-links, and reports workspace-wide status-consistency. SessionEnd cleans up the session's workflow-mode state. PostToolUse records edits and reports per-component status-consistency. Design principles — language choice, lifecycle symmetry, ordering, non-blocking, output channels — live in `references/hook-conventions.md`. Status transitions and their supersedes/discarded/revising cascades run synchronously from `scripts/transition_status.py` inside the invoking skill/agent — there are no status-writer hooks.

hooks.description: Record edited a4/*.md and report per-component status-consistency
Validate a4/*.md edited this session; exit 2 on violations to force fix
Delete this session's a4-edited record file
Delete this session's workflow-mode state file
Sweep orphan a4-edited record files older than 1 day
Init + sweep workflow-mode state, refresh UC implemented_by reverse-links, report workspace-wide status-consistency
